rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks to check a research passage whose core contribution framing depends on claims about inductive generalization and abstention under ambiguity.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is load-bearing because the artifact contains an apparent tension between fully inductive behavior and learned per-node embeddings.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central because the passage makes empirical and causal claims about selective accuracy and why the gain occurs.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of responsibility should preserve uncertainty where the short delegation does not specify a precise review mode.
rationale.RX.2: The checker must avoid endorsing claims stronger than what the reported setup and evidence support.
rationale.RX.3: The response should remain within checking the provided research artifact rather than performing unrelated rewriting or execution.
rationale.RX.4: The check should avoid downstream harm from misleading methodological or evaluation claims.
rationale.RX.5: Concerns should remain traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact.
